title: Privacy impact assessment for  the use of admin data  in the 2023 Census | Stats NZ
content:
## Privacy impact assessment for  the use of admin data  in the 2023 Census
05 December 2023, 12:00pm
Privacy impact assessment for the use of admin data in the 2023 Census identifies and assesses the key privacy risks associated with using administrative (admin) data in the production of the 2023 Census dataset.
Download the document below, or read the summary online.
- Privacy impact assessment for the use of admin data in the 2023 Census Adobe Acrobat PDF file, 650 KB

### Privacy impact assessment for the use of admin data in the 2023 Census
Adobe Acrobat PDF file, 650 KB
This PIA covers the use of admin data in the production of the 2023 Census dataset. This includes data already provided to Stats NZ to build the IDI, as well as additional supplies of data from the following organisations:
- Accident Compensation Corporation
- Department of Corrections
- Department of Internal Affairs
- Electoral Commission (new for 2023)
- Kāinga Ora – Homes and Communities
- Ministry of Business, Innovation and Employment (border movements)
- Ministry of Business, Innovation and Employment (tenancy services)
- Ministry of Education (study participation, highest qualifications)
- Manatū Hauora – Ministry of Health
- Ministry of Social Development
- New Zealand Customs Service
- Waka Kotahi – New Zealand Transport Agency.
This document does not cover the collection of 2023 Census responses.
ISBN 978-1-99-104962-9 (online)
Email census.communications@stats.govt.nz
